timeline.label: Tom Albanese appointed independent non-executive Chair after David Harquail steps to Chair Emeritus at AGM.
Available capital rises to $3.4B (cash, equity investments, undrawn revolver); record Q1 revenue and operating cash flow.
Quarterly dividend raised 16% to $0.44 per share ($1.76 annualized), 19th consecutive annual increase.
Agreed to acquire six-royalty portfolio from Victoria Gold Corp. (Yukon/Nevada); closing expected H1 2026.
FY2025 record: $1.82B revenue, $1.49B operating cash flow, 519k GEOs including 11,208 from Cobre Panamá.
Year-end available capital $3.1B; no debt; shareholders' equity $7.6B.
2026 GEO guidance 510–570k total GEOs (~90% precious); Cobre Panamá excluded from guidance band.
FY2024 operating cash flow $829.5M baseline used in depletion reserve proof vs FY2025 peak.
Cobre Panamá mine suspended; Franco-Nevada retains stream/royalty claims pending operator restart path.
IPO era: Franco-Nevada relisted as pure-play royalty company (Harquail leadership through 2026 chair transition).

industry.structure: Gold royalty/streaming is concentrated among a handful of listed players (Franco-Nevada, Wheaton, Royal Gold, Osisko); barriers are deal origination, balance sheet, and operator relationships rather than operating mines.
industry.share_shift: Franco-Nevada remains the largest gold royalty portfolio by scale; recent deals (Cascabel buy-back, Antapaccay with Glencore, Victoria Gold portfolio) extend precious-metal exposure while diversified oil/gas royalties add non-gold cash.
industry.trend: Record gold and silver prices in 2025–2026 lifted royalty cash flows; operators are spending exploration capital on Franco-Nevada's deep book while Cobre Panamá restart and stockpile processing remain the largest single asset swing factor.
